key_insight: RL post-training gains are surprisingly concentrated: training just one transformer layer can recover most of the benefit of full-parameter RL, often with the strongest effect in middle layers.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a far cheaper and more targeted way to adapt large models, reducing training cost, memory use, and update scope for RL post-training.
method: Define "layer contribution" as the fraction of full RL improvement recovered by training one layer in isolation.
Run layer-wise RL studies across transformer stacks instead of updating all parameters uniformly.
Compare rankings of important layers across models, tasks, and RL algorithms to test stability.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on seven models from Qwen3 and Qwen2.5 using three RL algorithms (GRPO, GiGPO, Dr. GRPO) across mathematical reasoning, code generation, and agentic decision-making tasks.
watch_item: Single-layer gains may be sensitive to the specific RL setup, so transferring this strategy to other model families or training regimes likely needs validation before deployment.
paper_title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
